premises.content: The bilayer architecture combining mesoscopic mp-TiO2-perovskite nanocomposite base with a planar perovskite upper layer (100-300 nm) effectively absorbs light and collects charges, merging advantages of mesoscopic and planar structures
premises.label: bilayer_architecture

premises.content: Substituting 10-15 mol% Br- for I- in MAPbI3 greatly improves stability in ambient atmosphere while the dual halide material demonstrates similar photovoltaic performance over the compositional range
premises.label: br_improves_ambient_stability

premises.content: DMSO in the MAI-PbI2-DMSO intermediate phase retards the rapid reaction between PbI2 and MAI during solvent evaporation, enabling controlled crystallization and uniform film formation
premises.label: dmpso_retards_reaction

premises.content: The MAI-PbI2-DMSO intermediate phase is a new crystalline compound (confirmed by XRD peaks at 6.55, 7.21, and 9.17 degrees and FTIR showing both N-H and S-O deformation modes) that converts to perovskite upon annealing at 130C
premises.label: intermediate_phase

premises.content: Poly(triarylamine) (PTAA) used as hole-transporting material achieves high performance in combination with the bilayer perovskite architecture, enabling the certified 16.2% PCE
premises.label: ptaa_htm

premises.content: Without toluene drop-casting, the film adopts a textile-like inhomogeneous layer that does not fully cover the substrate. The toluene drip is critically important for producing uniform, dense, and fully covering perovskite films
premises.label: toluene_critical
